      34 +  config value overrides the `agents.delivery_mode` column at
      35 +  mailman-startup time:
      36 +
      37 +  ```toml
      38 +  [agent.operator]
      39 +  delivery-mode = "mailbox-only"
      40 +  ```
      41 +
      42 +  Lets operators who manage state via config (rather than via the
      43 +  `claude-msg register` / MCP `tmux-msg.register` paths) declare the
      44 +  mode without writing to the DB. The register-time CLI / MCP path
      45 +  still writes to the DB column; the TOML knob is the OVERRIDE at
      46 +  mailman-startup time — the DB column is the long-term default;
      47 +  config wins per-run.
      48 +
      49 +  Validation: invalid mode values from config log a `WARN
      50 +  config_delivery_mode_invalid` and the DB column wins (fail-loud,
      51 +  not fail-stop — a typo in `/etc/tmux-msg/config.toml` doesn't
      52 +  silently break the mailman).
      53 +
      54 +  New `config.ResolveString` helper centralizes the per-string-field
      55 +  precedence chain (sister to `ResolveBool` / `ResolveDuration`); the
      56 +  delivery-mode knob is the first string-typed field, designed
      57 +  forward-compatibly for additional string knobs.
      58 +
      59 +  Surfaces:
      60 +  - **TOML**: `delivery-mode` per-agent or `[defaults]` block
      61 +  - **`ResolvedView.DeliveryMode string`** surfaced in
      62 +    `claude-msg config show` so operators can verify the resolved
      63 +    value without tracing through TOML manually
      64 +  - **No CLI flag**: the register-time CLI already covers the operator
      65 +    workflow; adding a `--delivery-mode` override to `serve` would
      66 +    duplicate the `register` surface without adding a use case
      67 +
      68  - **Pre-paste safety check against popup-as-Unknown destruction
      69    (#105 Half 2).** New mailman safety net: immediately before each
      70    paste-and-Enter delivery, the mailman takes one final `AgentState`

  Called forgejo (ctrl+o to expand)

  respond, I'll have a popup-render to capture from this very session.

  Called semaphore, forgejo 2 times (ctrl+o to expand)

● Now for #133 — I need to capture an AskUserQuestion popup variant. Asking you a question opens the popup IN this Quartermaster pane (%5), which gives me a popup-render to capture from a
  sibling-pane (e.g., I can ask Bosun to tmux capture-pane -p -t %5 while the popup is open below):
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ☐ Capture setup

Ready for the popup-capture coordination?

❯ 1. Yes — leave this question open while I capture (Recommended)
     Don't select an option immediately. The popup stays rendered in my pane while I trigger another chamber (e.g., Bosun via bus) to run `tmux capture-pane -p -t %5` against my pane,
     capturing the live AskUserQuestion render. Then you select 'Continue' below to dismiss.
  2. Continue — select after capture is done
     Use this option to dismiss the popup once you've heard 'capture complete' from me. The capture happens out-of-band via Bosun's bus tool call.
  3. Type something.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  4. Chat about this

Enter to select · ↑/↓ to navigate · Esc to cancel
